data.title: Apple Stock Forecast — Should You Catch this Falling Knife?
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) shares have dropped nearly 8% over the past five days after the company delayed its highly anticipated Siri AI upgrades. Investors are wary of repeated Siri setbacks and Apple's late AI entry.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A Bloomberg report noted that Apple's Siri update has hit testing snags, potentially delaying several features. Apple originally targeted iOS 26.4 for the March release but now plans phased rollouts across multiple updates. Some are expected with iOS 26.5 in May or even with iOS 27 in September.

Here's What Analysts Say

Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani maintained his Buy rating and $330 price target (29% upside potential) despite news of the delay. The five-star analyst noted that testing issues have forced Siri to stick with its existing ChatGPT integration rather than Apple's new tech. He expects gradual feature rollouts starting mid-year.

iOS 26.5 will introduce a new web search tool and custom image generation. Additionally, iOS 27 brings a major AI overhaul, possibly integrating Google's (GOOGL) Gemini models. Daryanani also highlighted that Apple's Q1FY26 results beat expectations on sales and earnings, boosted by strong Services growth and low memory chip costs.

For the March quarter, Apple had guided for 13%-16% revenue growth, above prior estimates, backed by solid iPhone supply and continued Services momentum. The company is focusing on data privacy and smooth integration for its upcoming AI features.

Similarly, Robert W. Baird analyst William Power also reiterated his Buy rating and $300 price target, suggesting 17.3% upside potential. His price target suggests a 35x 2026 EPS forecast, sitting at the high end of Apple's post-COVID range (25-35x). AAPL stock valuation is also above peers in tech/consumer staples, but is justified by solid execution, rising Services revenue, ecosystem strength, and lower capital needs.

Meanwhile, Power sees risks such as component pricing and supply chain issues, regulatory and geopolitical pressures, competition from Google, Samsung (SSNLF), Microsoft (MSFT) as well as macroeconomic headwinds, changing consumer tastes, and high valuation.

Is AAPL a Good Stock to Buy?

Despite Siri delays causing an 8% five-day AAPL drop, analysts like Daryanani and Power maintain Buy ratings, viewing it as a calculated risk for long-term investors given strong Services growth and phased AI rollouts.

On TipRanks, Apple stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 17 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Apple price target of $307.55 implies 20.2% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, AAPL shares have gained 4.6%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Micron's (MU) AI Supercycle May Be In Its Early Innings
data.description:
The semiconductor niche, and the tech industry in general, has always been cyclical, but every few decades, something big happens that really changes the direction of travel. I think we are in one of those moments now, and Micron Technology (MU) is right in the middle of it. The stock has received much hype lately, but I still feel like market participants are only starting to understand what is actually going on.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Computer memory used to be treated like a basic commodity, important but replaceable, and mostly about price. That narrative does not really fit anymore. In the AI-driven world, memory makes everything possible. For this reason (as well as MU's ownership structure), I am bullish on the stock and the broader AI theme.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: Why the Pentagon Is Racing to Deploy New Counter-Drone Sensors
data.description:
The Pentagon is moving quickly to improve its ability to detect small drones that could threaten military bases across the United States. According to a new solicitation from the Defense Innovation Unit, companies must be ready to demonstrate their technology by spring 2026 at the Yuma Proving Ground in Arizona. Because officials see this as an urgent threat, selected companies may have 30 days or less between being notified and carrying out the live demonstration. The project, called "Counter UAS Sensing for Homeland and Mobile Defense," is designed to protect both domestic bases and military units on the battlefield.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The first part of the program focuses on defending fixed sites inside the U.S. Importantly, the required sensors must be able to detect Group 1 drones (the smallest category, weighing under 20 pounds) from at least 2 kilometers away. Ideally, they should also be capable of identifying larger Group 2 and Group 3 drones, which can weigh up to 1,320 pounds. The systems also need to integrate with government counter-drone fire control platforms and operate safely in populated areas and crowded airspace without disrupting lawful signals.

Moreover, the second part of the project focuses on mobile protection for military units that are constantly moving. These sensors must be able to detect small drones that are flying below 50 meters while keeping a low physical and electronic signature so enemy forces cannot easily target them. For investors looking to gain exposure to increased defense spending in counter-drone and military technology, ETFs such as the following offer diversified access to major contractors likely to benefit:

  • SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR)
  • Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA)
  • iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA)

Which Defense ETF Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three defense ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that XAR has the most room to run. In fact, XAR's price target of $315.24 per share implies 16.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 'Don't Go Bargain Hunting': Top Investor Throws Cold Water on Archer Aviation Stock (ACHR)
data.description:
While the sky might be the limit for Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR), for the past few months the eVTOL manufacturer's stock hasn't been flying high. Since hitting a 52-week peak in mid-October, the share price has lost roughly half of its value.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Much of the worries surrounding the pre-revenue company revolve around Archer's uncertain flightpath. Beyond the regulatory hurdles the company needs to clear prior to ferrying commercial passengers, a series of dilutive capital raises – the last, a $650 million equity raise announced at the same time as its Q3 2025 earnings release – have left investors none too pleased.

Now, a new wrench is mucking up the gears, following a biting critique from Culper Research. Among other allegations made by the short-selling firm, Culper is accusing Archer of not engaging in a single ground or air test for the past three months. The social media post hit like a stone, dropping ACHR down another few notches.

Still, the company is playing in what could become a very lucrative sandbox. Morgan Stanley, for instance, has estimated that the low-altitude market could be worth up to $9 trillion by 2050.

Archer has also worked to build credibility through strategic partnerships with established aviation players in the U.S. and internationally, helping to keep enthusiasm alive despite the volatility.

Yet, top investor Adam Spatacco is choosing to stay on the sidelines for now, citing risks that continue to cloud the company's near-term outlook.

"While Archer's ability to attract big tech and the military is exciting, the company hasn't made a ton of progress penetrating its multitrillion-dollar opportunity," explains the 5-star investor, who is among the top 2% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Spatacco acknowledges that ACHR is a tough one to assess, especially given that it has yet to enjoy any measurable sales. He points out that the last time Archer shone some light on its sales figures was over a year ago, when it posted a backlog of more than $6 billion.

That's not the same as cash in the bank, however, the investor acknowledges.

What seems to be driving ACHR's valuation is the narrative spinning around the company, both for the better and for the worse. Spatacco argues that announcements of collaborations with a new commercial partner or exciting tech company causes a surge of retail and day traders to snap up shares of ACHR.

"My point to all of this is that investing in Archer feels more aligned with owning equity in a speculative start-up rather than a durable business with predictable cash flow," adds Spatacco.

Questions such as aircraft delivery, order payment, and recognizable revenues have yet to be answered, leaving too much uncertainty for Spatacco. The investor predicts things will fall farther before a potential recovery beckons.

"I'd pass on owning Archer stock at the moment," concludes Spatacco. (To watch Adam Spatacco's track record, click here)

Wall Street's coverage has been relatively quiet lately, with just 3 analysts weighing in on ACHR over the past quarter. Still, the outlook tilts constructive: 2 analysts rate the stock a Buy and one recommends Hold, giving Archer a Moderate Buy consensus. With a 12-month average price target of $11.50, the analysts see potential upside of ~70% from current levels. (See ACHR stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

These include pharmaceutical companies that make medications; medical device firms that develop products ranging from artificial hearts and pacemakers to artificial limbs and hearing aids; and biotechnology concerns that are involved in cutting-edge technologies such as genomics.

Healthcare exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that focus on specific sectors of healthcare can be a great way to boost an investment portfolio, especially funds that are geared towards new technologies, treatments and medicines.

Some great specialty ETFs for investors to consider include the iShares U.S. Medical Devices ETF (IHI), which holds stocks of leading medical device companies such as Boston Scientific (BSX), Intuitive Surgical (ISRG), and Medtronic (MDT).

There's also the iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (IHE) that owns stocks of leading pharma concerns such as Eli Lilly & Co. (LLY), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and Merck & Co. (MRK). data.title: Top Analysts Are Bullish on Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2026 on February 25. NVDA stock has risen 35% over the past year but is down nearly 2% so far in 2026 amid concerns about growing competition and fears about an AI (artificial intelligence) bubble. Nevertheless, top Wall Street analysts remain bullish on Nvidia due to its solid execution, robust demand for its AI GPUs (graphics processing units), and continued innovation.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Nvidia to report earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, reflecting 71% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise 67% to $65.58 billion.

data.title: "Big Bet on Efficiency" United Airlines (UAL) Expands GE Aerospace Partnership with 300 GEnx Engines
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) is deepening its partnership with GE Aerospace (GE). The airline announced it has selected GEnx engines from GE Aerospace to power its new fleet of Boeing (BA) 787 Dreamliners. This agreement makes United the largest GEnx operator, with its total GEnx-powered fleet now exceeding 200 aircraft.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The deal includes 300 GEnx engines for new Dreamliner deliveries and additional spare engines to support fleet maintenance.

The move reflects United's push to modernize its wide-body fleet with more fuel-efficient aircraft as international travel demand continues to rebound. GE's GEnx engines are known for high reliability (99.98% dispatch rate), 15% better fuel efficiency, increased durability, and reduced noise.

For United, the decision supports its broader strategy to cut operating costs and meet sustainability targets while expanding long-haul routes.

Is UAL a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, UAL stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 17 Buys and one Hold assigned in the last three months. At $142.32, the average United Airlines stock price target implies a 30.22% upside potential.

data.title: Swiss Bank UBS (UBS) Expects Two Rate Cuts from the Fed This Year
data.description:
Swiss bank UBS Group (UBS) says it expects the U.S. Federal Reserve to cut interest rates twice this year.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts at UBS say that signs tariff-driven inflation has peaked should enable the U.S. central bank to lower interest rates further in coming months. The outlook from UBS comes after recent data showed that inflation in America stands at an annualized 2.4%.

While that's above the Fed's 2% inflation target, it is a relatively benign reading that has bolstered bets that the central bank will next cut interest rates in June of this year. The trendsetting Federal Funds Rate currently sits in a range of 3.50% to 3.75%.

Improving U.S. Conditions

"We believe the Fed remains on track to ease further, and we expect two 25-basis-point rate cuts between June and September. This backdrop is favorable for equities, bonds, and gold, in our view," the UBS analysts wrote in a note to clients.

The Swiss bank adds that comments from Kevin Warsh, U.S. President Donald Trump's nominee to take over the central bank, that greater productivity will temper price gains show a preference for looser monetary policy, which supports lower rates.

Is UBS Stock a Buy?

UBS stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on seven Buy, two Hold, and two Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average UBS price target of $51.98 implies 23.79% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'AI Agents Not a Threat to Palantir' Says Analyst — Double Upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Freedom Capital Markets analyst Almas Almaganbetov issued a rare double upgrade for Palantir Technologies (PLTR) after its robust Q4FY25 results and upbeat guidance. He upgraded PLTR from Sell to Buy, stating that he does not see AI agents as a threat to Palantir's long-term business model. Almaganbetov also set a price target of $170, implying 29.4% upside potential from current levels. PLTR stock gained 1.7% on Friday, following the upgrade.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The big data analytics company reported Q4 earnings per share of $0.25, surpassing the $0.23 consensus. Revenue hit $1.407 billion, up 70% from the prior year and ahead of the $1.34 billion forecast. Commercial revenue surged 137% year-over-year to $507 million, while government revenue grew 66% to $570 million.

Analyst Highlights AIP Growth Story

Almaganbetov noted that Palantir's Q4 results far exceeded both management's guidance and his estimates. The company's outlook for Q1 and FY26 also topped consensus. He noted that this strong forecast signals Palantir management's confidence that demand for AI infrastructure is durable and structural, not cyclical.

Almaganbetov remains particularly impressed by the growth in the company's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP), which is driving U.S. market growth by accelerating sales and turning pilots into major contracts. He called the recent sharp pullback "unjustified," given continued hypergrowth and the underlying strength in AIP. He also raised his revenue forecasts for fiscal 2026 and 2027, reiterating that new AI agents pose no long-term risk.

Not all analysts agree. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria maintained his "Hold" rating, viewing PLTR shares as "expensive." Meanwhile, he dismissed concerns from Big Short investor Michael Burry's recent 10,000-page thesis, which raises concerns about the sustainability of the current AI investment cycle. Following the release of his newsletter, PLTR stock plunged 6.7% on February 12.

Is Palantir a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street remains divided on Palantir's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, PLTR stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and two Sell ratings. The average Palantir Technologies price target of $192.38 implies 46.4% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PLTR shares have gained 10.3%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
